name: toon-format
description: TOON (Token-Oriented Object Notation) encoding for LLM-efficient data representation. 30-60% token savings vs JSON for structured data.
capabilities: Encodes tabular data in TOON format with 30-60% token savings vs JSON
Validates TOON syntax using the official @toon-format/cli tool
Provides complete format rules for proper TOON syntax
Auto-fixes common TOON formatting errors with documented scripts
Supports nested objects, multi-line strings, and explicit array counts
Creates efficient documentation indexes and manifests for LLMs
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with format rules and validation examples. No executable code, scripts, or network operations. All 142 static findings are false positives from the scanner misinterpreting documentation code blocks as executable shell commands and example hash values as cryptographic operations.
valueStatement: JSON wastes tokens in LLM prompts. TOON reduces token usage by 30-60% while keeping data human-readable. Transform tabular data into a compact format optimized for AI consumption.
userTitle: Format data efficiently with TOON notation
limitations: Only suitable for uniform tabular data structures
Requires the official TOON CLI for validation
Not ideal for deeply nested or non-uniform data

useCases.title: Optimize LLM prompt data
useCases.description: Reduce token costs by encoding documentation and indexes in TOON format before sending to language models.

useCases.title: Create efficient documentation indexes
useCases.description: Build structured indexes and manifests that consume fewer tokens when processed by AI systems.

useCases.title: Format tabular data for AI processing
useCases.description: Transform CSV-like datasets into TOON for more efficient LLM consumption and analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Convert JSON to TOON
promptTemplates.scenario: You have JSON data that needs TOON formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this JSON array to TOON format with proper syntax: use explicit count, 2-space indentation, and add blank line after arrays. Here's the JSON: [paste your data]

promptTemplates.title: Validate TOON file
promptTemplates.scenario: Check if your TOON syntax is correct
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate this TOON file and fix any syntax errors: missing counts, wrong indentation, or formatting issues. Show the corrected version: [paste your TOON]

promptTemplates.title: Design tabular structure
promptTemplates.scenario: Plan data layout for TOON encoding
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me design a TOON format for this data. Show how to structure it as tabular arrays with proper column definitions: [describe your data]

promptTemplates.title: Batch convert documents
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a script to convert these documentation files to TOON format. Include validation and error handling: [list your files]

bestPractices: Always validate with npx @toon-format/cli --decode before using in production
Design data structures for tabular representation when possible
Keep JSON as canonical source and generate TOON for LLM transport
antiPatterns: Using TOON for deeply nested or irregular data structures
Including comments or YAML-style lists in TOON files
Forgetting blank lines after tabular arrays

faq.question: Is TOON compatible with standard JSON parsers?
faq.answer: No, TOON requires specialized parsers. Use official libraries for TypeScript (@toon-format/toon) and Python (python-toon).

faq.question: What are the token savings compared to JSON?
faq.answer: TOON typically saves 30-60% tokens by using compact tabular syntax and removing unnecessary punctuation.

faq.question: Can I use TOON for configuration files?
faq.answer: No, use JSON instead. TOON is optimized for LLM prompts, not for tools that expect standard JSON.

faq.question: How do I handle validation errors?
faq.answer: Run the auto-fix scripts in order from validation-tools.md, then re-validate with npx @toon-format/cli --decode.

faq.question: Does TOON support all JSON features?
faq.answer: No, TOON lacks comments, YAML-style lists, and multiline strings. It's designed for tabular data efficiency.

faq.question: When should I choose TOON over JSON?
faq.answer: Use TOON for uniform tabular data sent to LLMs. Use JSON for configuration, APIs, or programmatic data manipulation.
